summary: First testing round for Tacos POS. Backend (14 pytest cases) and frontend (POS + Admin flows) all pass against the live preview URL. Tip business rule (efectivo ignores tip, tarjeta/transferencia accepts it) verified end-to-end. Product price editing in Admin reflects immediately in POS.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); consider migrating to lifespan handlers (FastAPI >=0.110).

Admin endpoint returns a static token 'admin-session' and other endpoints are NOT auth-protected. Acceptable for the current MVP scope but should be flagged: anyone hitting /api/products PUT/POST/DELETE or /api/sales can mutate data without auth.
POS.jsx: handleCharge sends `tip` even for efectivo; backend correctly ignores it, but client could also zero it out for consistency.
Dashboard polling uses Promise.all of 3 endpoints every 5s with no cancellation when tab is hidden; not critical for MVP.
Sales total response computes total = subtotal + tip; rounding done with round(_, 2) which is fine for integer MXN amounts but watch for IEEE 754 surprises if decimals are introduced.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded products. Tests created ~5 sales of the current day during execution. No cleanup performed (sales endpoints have no DELETE).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers /api/products (list/update/toggle), /api/sales (efectivo/tarjeta/transferencia + tip ignored on efectivo + empty cart 400), /api/dashboard structure, and /api/admin/login. Frontend was validated in mobile viewport 390x844 across POS (add to cart, switch payment methods, tip flow, charge) and Admin (invalid+valid login, KPIs, payment cards, sales table populated, price edit reflects in POS). Existing sales in DB increase as tests run; expect /api/dashboard counts to grow on retest.
